pokemon:azurill.name: Azurill
pokemon:azurill.summary: Azurill’s tail is large and bouncy. It is packed full of the nutrients this Pokémon needs to grow. Azurill can be seen bouncing and playing on its big, rubbery tail.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Azurill’s tail is large and bouncy. It is packed full of the nutrients this Pokémon needs to grow. Azurill can be seen bouncing and playing on its big, rubbery tail.
Its tail bounces like a rubber ball. It flings that tail around to fight opponents bigger than itself.
It swings its large, nutrient-filled tail around to fight opponents bigger than itself.
A Pokémon that lives by water. It moves quickly on land by bouncing on its big tail.
Its tail, which is packed with nutrition, is very bouncy like a rubber ball. On sunny days they gather at the edge of water and splash about for fun.
It battles by flinging around its tail, which is bigger than its body. The tail is a flotation device in water.
Its tail is packed full of the nutrients it needs to grow.
It battles by flinging around its tail, which is bigger than its body. The tail is a flotation device in water.

Azurill spins its tail as if it were a lasso, then hurls it far. The momentum of the throw sends its body flying, too. Using this unique action, one of these Pokémon managed to hurl itself a record 33 feet.
Its tail is filled with nutrients necessary for growth. It plays by bouncing on its tail.
Its tail bounces like a rubber ball. It flings that tail around to fight opponents bigger than itself.

AZURILL spins its tail as if it were a lasso, then hurls it far. The momentum of the throw sends its body flying, too. Using this unique action, one of these POKéMON managed to hurl itself a record 33 feet.
AZURILL’s tail is large and bouncy. It is packed full of the nutrients this POKéMON needs to grow. AZURILL can be seen bouncing and playing on its big, rubbery tail.
Although Azurill are normally docile, an angry one will swing around the big ball on its tail and try to smash its opponents.
Its tail is packed full of the nutrients it needs to grow.
The ball on Azurill’s tail bounces like a rubber ball, and it’s full of the nutrients the Pokémon needs to grow.
Its tail bounces like a rubber ball. It flings that tail around to fight opponents bigger than itself.
It swings its large, nutrient-filled tail around to fight opponents bigger than itself.
A Pokémon that lives by water. It moves quickly on land by bouncing on its big tail.
It swings its large, nutrient-filled tail around to fight opponents bigger than itself.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Azurill’s tail is large and bouncy. It is packed full of the nutrients this Pokémon needs to grow. Azurill can be seen bouncing and playing on its big, rubbery tail.

pokemon:azumarill.name: Azumarill
pokemon:azumarill.summary: Azumarill can make balloons out of air. It makes these air balloons if it spots a drowning Pokémon. The air balloons enable the Pokémon in trouble to breathe.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Azumarill can make balloons out of air. It makes these air balloons if it spots a drowning Pokémon. The air balloons enable the Pokémon in trouble to breathe.
It can spend all day in water, since it can inhale and store a large volume of air.
Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of things in water and tell what they are.
The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it's in the water.
It lives in rivers and lakes. In water, its coloring and patterns trick the vision of foes.
It lives in water virtually all day long. Its body color and pattern act as camouflage that makes it tough for enemies to spot in water.
When it plays in water, it rolls up its elongated ears to prevent their insides from getting wet.
By keeping still and listening in­ tently, it can tell what is in even wild, fast- moving rivers.
By keeping still and listening intently, it can tell what is in even wild, fast-moving rivers.
By keeping still and listening intently, it can even tell what is in wild, fast-moving rivers.
Azumarill’s long ears are indispensable sensors. By focusing its hearing, this Pokémon can identify what kinds of prey are around, even in rough and fast-running rivers.
Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of living things on riverbeds.
It can spend all day in water, since it can inhale and store a large volume of air.
AZUMARILL’s long ears are indispensable sensors. By focusing its hearing, this POKéMON can identify what kinds of prey are around, even in rough and fast-running rivers.
AZUMARILL can make balloons out of air. It makes these air balloons if it spots a drowning POKéMON. The air balloons enable the POKéMON in trouble to breathe.
These Pokémon create air-filled bubbles. When Azurill play in rivers, Azumarill will cover them with these bubbles.
When it plays in water, it rolls up its elongated ears to prevent their insides from get­ ting wet.
When it plays in water, it rolls up its elongated ears to prevent their insides from getting wet.
It spends most of its time in the water. On sunny days, Azumarill floats on the surface of the water and sunbathes.
It can spend all day in water, since it can inhale and store a large volume of air.
Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of things in water and tell what they are.
Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of living things on riverbeds.
The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it’s in the water.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Azumarill can make balloons out of air. It makes these air balloons if it spots a drowning Pokémon. The air balloons enable the Pokémon in trouble to breathe.

pokemon:marill.name: Marill
pokemon:marill.summary: When fishing for food at the edge of a fast-running stream, Marill wraps its tail around the trunk of a tree. This Pokémon’s tail is flexible and configured to stretch.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: When fishing for food at the edge of a fast-running stream, Marill wraps its tail around the trunk of a tree. This Pokémon’s tail is flexible and configured to stretch.
The oil-filled end of its tail floats on water. It keeps Marill from drowning even in a strong current.
The oil-filled tail functions as a buoy, so it’s fine even in rivers with strong currents.
The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry, even when it plays in the water.
Using its tail as a float, it dives underwater. It likes eating plants that grow on river bottoms.
Its body is covered with water-repellent fur. Because of the fur, it can swim through water at high speed without being slowed by the water’s resistance.
The end of its tail serves as a buoy that keeps it from drowning, even in a vicious current.
The tip of its tail, which con­ tains oil that is lighter than wa­ ter, lets it swim without drowning.
The tip of its tail, which contains oil that is lighter than water, lets it swim without drowning.
The tip of its tail, which contains oil that is lighter than water, lets it swim without drowning.

Marill’s oil-filled tail acts much like a life preserver. If you see just its tail bobbing on the water’s surface, it’s a sure indication that this Pokémon is diving beneath the water to feed on aquatic plants.
The tip of its tail is filled with oil that is lighter than water, so it acts as a float.
The oil-filled end of its tail floats on water. It keeps MARILL from drowning even in a strong current.

MARILL’s oil-filled tail acts much like a life preserver. If you see just its tail bobbing on the water’s surface, it’s a sure indication that this POKéMON is diving beneath the water to feed on aquatic plants.
When fishing for food at the edge of a fast-running stream, MARILL wraps its tail around the trunk of a tree. This POKéMON’s tail is flexible and configured to stretch.
Even after Marill swims in a cold sea, its water- repellent fur dries almost as soon as Marill leaves the water. That’s why this Pokémon is never cold.
The end of its tail serves as a buoy that keeps it from drowning, even in a vicious current.
The end of its tail serves as a buoy that keeps it from drowning, even in a vicious current.
This Pokémon uses its round tail as a float. The ball of Marill’s tail is filled with nutrients that have been turned into an oil.
The oil-filled end of its tail floats on water. It keeps Marill from drowning even in a strong current.
The oil-filled tail functions as a buoy, so it’s fine even in rivers with strong currents.
The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry, even when it plays in the water.
The tip of its tail is filled with oil that is lighter than water, so it acts as a float.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: When fishing for food at the edge of a fast-running stream, Marill wraps its tail around the trunk of a tree. This Pokémon’s tail is flexible and configured to stretch.
